_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for ASML Holding (ASML). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: Wisesheets — not yet pulled. Agent must search for TTM financials before running DCF.
_currency_note: ASML reports in EUR. Convert to USD at prevailing rate when populating.
story_narrative: ASML is the sole supplier of EUV lithography machines — without them there are no chips below 7nm. This is a one-company monopoly in the most critical tool in semiconductor manufacturing. Every leading-edge fab (TSMC, Samsung, Intel) must buy from ASML. High NA EUV (for 2nm and below) extends the monopoly through 2030+. The risk is China export controls permanently remove a $2–3B/yr revenue stream (~15% of sales). ASML's backlog structure (40% upfront cash, ships 2–3 years out) makes revenue highly predictable. The TSMC CapEx cycle is a direct leading indicator for ASML machine orders.
story_revenue_cagr: 16% reflects EUV volume ramp (TSMC N2/A16, Samsung 2nm, Intel 18A) plus High NA EUV introduction at premium ASPs (~€350M/machine vs €200M for standard EUV).
story_target_margin: 34% reflects scale leverage on EBIT as revenue doubles. Currently ~30–32% EBIT margin; High NA machines carry higher margin than prior generation.
story_growth_years: 10 years — two full EUV generations (current EUV → High NA → beyond). ASML's roadmap extends to 2030 and beyond with backlog visibility.
story_stable_growth: 3% — once leading-edge transition slows, replacement and upgrade cycle sustains modest growth aligned with global semiconductor capex.
story_stable_margin: 28% stable EBIT margin — service/install base revenue becomes more significant proportion; lower margin than new machine sales but recurring.
story_s2c_high: 1.5x — ASML is asset-light for a capital goods company; the value is in IP (EUV optics, source technology). Physical CapEx is moderate relative to revenue.
story_s2c_stable: 1.8x — service and upgrade revenue requires very little incremental capital investment.
story_beta: 0.95 — cyclical but backlog visibility reduces realized volatility. Dutch company with EUR/USD exposure adds slight currency risk.
story_rd_cap: 10-year amortization — EUV IP (optics design, laser source, lithography algorithms) has useful life of a full technology generation, typically 8–12 years.
story_uncertainty: Medium — monopoly position is clear but China export controls, customer concentration (TSMC ~30% of revenue), and EUR/USD currency exposure justify medium uncertainty.
